title: Implement full #2128 session-log lane

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Implement the full #2128 lane in PR #2141, including all child issues before using auto-close references.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not close #2128 from partial work; keep changes bounded to the #2128 child issue surfaces and required generated command contracts.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Exact implementation details, focused tests, validation order, and PR body wording, as long as every lane child is covered.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A child issue requires a broader redesign, external service dependency, or irreversible migration outside the lane scope.
canonical_core.next_action: Implement remaining #2128 lane children, then run focused proof and update PR #2141 with complete auto-close references.
proof_expectations: Focused tests for session-log analyze/index/path modes, structured CLI errors, modules sections, Memory route/capture recovery, proof/closeout semantics, and #2123 proof-file wording.
Regenerate/check command surfaces after CLI contract changes.
Run lane-relevant workspace validation and document any unrelated known failures separately.
completion_criteria: #2129 session-log analyzer accepts current log and explicit path/id and reports requested counts, failures, duplicate outputs, parsed packet kinds, and friction candidates.
#2130 session-log sidecar/index records command/output metadata and content-aware artifact summaries with duplicate output reuse.
#2131 retryable CLI usage errors produce structured JSON suggestions and repeated-failure hints.
#2132 session logging supports configured path modes that avoid leaking home/python paths when configured.
#2133 modules output is compact by default and supports section-addressable drilldown while retaining verbose output.
#2134 Memory route/capture reports weak-signal recovery options and low-confidence ownership alternatives.
#2136 implement/proof/closeout semantics distinguish blockers, proof tiers, archive/closure options, and successful help logging.
#2123 proof-file rejection wording names the rejected path and rule.
PR #2141 body auto-closes #2128 and all implemented child issues only after proof.
